texto: American attitudes toward teachers are frankly incoherent. We want outstanding people to enter the field but provide little incentive to do so. We expect teachers to be able to educate every child, including one for whom the obstacles to learning originate outside the school. They should be able to do this without adequate training, having figured it out on their own.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Mark Seidenberg sobre temas ligados a educacao. A afirmação “American attitudes toward teachers are frankly incoherent. We want outstanding people to enter th…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, education, teaching, útil em momentos de dia-chuvoso e teaching.
